repository.name: red-run

commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use Write Without Response and skip redundant BLE lookups (v0.3.0)

Two bugs caused sensors to go unavailable:

1. write_gatt_char(response=True) hangs 10+ seconds through ESPHome
   BT proxies — the ATT Write Response doesn't reliably traverse the
   proxy even though the device processes the command (notifications
   arrive). Switched to response=False (fire-and-forget writes).

2. async_ble_device_from_address() was called every poll, even when
   already connected. If the scanner missed a recent advertisement,
   this falsely marked sensors unavailable despite a healthy connection.
   Now only called when establishing a new connection.

Also: added BleakClient disconnect callback for instant drop detection,
reduced retry delay from 5s to 2s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
task: adj-036.1 Backend epic subtree graph filtering

Add server-side filtering to GET /api/beads/graph when epicId is provided.
Previously epicId was a client-side hint that did nothing server-side.

Now when epicId is set, the endpoint returns only:
- The epic itself
- Its parent (any bead whose dependencies include the epicId)
- All descendants recursively (children, grandchildren, etc.)
- Only edges between those filtered nodes

Implementation:
- New pure function filterGraphToEpicSubtree() in beads-dependency.ts
- Integrated into getBeadsGraph() in beads-queries.ts
- 12 new tests covering subtree filtering, parent inclusion, deep nesting,
  circular deps, missing epic, and no-children edge cases
- All 33 existing graph tests continue to pass (no regression)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Apple Calendar / iCal subscription export

- GET /api/export/ical-token — generates and returns a secret per-family token
  (lazily creates ical_token column on families table if missing)
- DELETE /api/export/ical-token — regenerates token, revoking the old feed URL
- GET /api/export/ical/[token] — public iCal feed (no auth, token is the secret);
  returns last 60 days + next 365 days of family events as RFC 5545 iCalendar,
  excluding driver events (__ride_driver__) and respecting recurrence rules
- Settings page: Apple Calendar Subscription section with copy-URL button,
  inline instructions, and revoke/regenerate link

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Day 2+3 — LightGBM signal layer, expanded universe, combined backtest

Day 2:
- Expand backtest universe to 25 S&P 100 symbols across 5 sectors
- Momentum Sharpe improves 1.447 → 1.504; max drawdown 31.5% → 19.0%
- Implement LGBMSignalModel + build_training_dataset() in features.py
- Train LightGBM on 29,925 rows; mean CV AUC 0.5201; top feature: volatility_60d
- Validate Alpaca paper trading: BUY 1 SPY accepted (order c1a2c7c9)

Day 3:
- Wire LightGBM into brain.py as confirmation gate (threshold=0.55)
- BUY signals blocked before Claude API if proba < 0.55 (cost + quality)
- SELL signals always bypass gate — exits never blocked
- Add init_lgbm_filter(), apply_lgbm_gate() to brain.py
- Combined backtest (train 2020-22, test 2023-24): filter −0.11 Sharpe in bull
  market — key finding: LightGBM gate is regime-dependent, not universally additive
- Fix sklearn feature-name warning: final model and predict_proba use named DataFrames
- 16/16 tests passing; 5 new tests for gate logic in tests/agent/test_brain.py

Auto-match bot guesser to waiting rooms after 3 min

If a PM agent's room has no guesser after 3 minutes, the rooms page
triggers POST /api/rooms/automatch which atomically assigns a Gemini
bot guesser. The bot asks yes/no questions, polls for the human PM's
answers (up to 2.5 min per question), then submits a solution.

commit.message:
FIX: Correct equipment IDs for FM consumption display

Equipment IDs in BRS facility have 'BRS_' prefix: BRS_BRSFM01, BRS_BRSFM02
Code was trying to access consumption with unprefixed IDs: BROSFM01, BROSFM02

Changes:
- Line 588-589: Use correct equipment IDs to fetch FM consumption from fmConsumptionMap
- Line 786-788: Create rows with correct equipment IDs (internal) but keep display label as BROSFM01/BROSFM02

This should now display actual FM consumption values in the production plan table,
allowing users to quickly spot clinker routing issues per finish mill.

commit.message:
fix(modal+gedcom): confirm modal z-index above compare + GEDCOM cache resilience

1. Confirm modal z-[9997] → z-[10002] so Merge confirmation overlays
   Compare modal (z-[10000]). Resolves Codex PR #6 intent.
2. GEDCOM cache no longer permanently caches empty results on DB error.
   Previously, a transient Supabase failure would cache [] forever,
   causing "Linked to: Unknown" for all GEDCOM-linked identities.
3. Added z-index ordering regression test.

commit.message:
Phase 2: Auth, Stripe billing, and AI key management

Frontend:
- AuthContext: real Supabase auth path wired (mock still default)
- ToastContext: toast notification system (success/error/warning/info)
- Signup: calls POST /api/auth/signup, handles mock session or Stripe redirect
- Settings: full AI key management UI (save, mask display, test, show/hide)
- api.ts: axios instance with auth token interceptor and 401 redirect
- supabase.ts: Supabase client singleton

Backend:
- auth.py: full signup flow (mock → session; real → Supabase + Stripe Checkout)
- auth.py: GET /me pulls from Supabase users table in real mode
- auth.py: POST /create-portal-session opens Stripe Customer Portal
- webhooks.py: Stripe webhook handles checkout.session.completed
  (creates Smartlead client, generates + encrypts scoped API key),
  subscription.updated, subscription.deleted
- settings.py: GET/POST /ai-keys with AES-256 encryption at rest;
  POST /ai-keys/test makes real minimal API call to Claude or OpenAI
- supabase_client.py: Supabase service-role admin client utility
- Encryption: key format validation (sk-ant- / sk-) before saving

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
